reforms.description: The Second Community Support Framework (CSF II) and the Operational Programme for Research and Technology (EPET II) aim to facilitate convergence with EU partners by upgrading infrastructure, stimulating private-sector investment, and supporting programs in health, education, and technology innovation with a combined budget of ECU 580 million for the period 1994-1999.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The reforms are designed to enhance infrastructure and stimulate innovation, which are critical for economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: Both programs have been actively implemented since their inception.
reforms.importance_rationale: Both reforms represent significant structural changes aimed at enhancing R&D and innovation capabilities.
reforms.source_quote: The Second Community Support Framework, (CSF II) is designed to facilitate convergence with the EU partners through a programme centred on upgrading the economy's infrastructure, stimulating private-sector industrial investment, as well as supporting programmes in the areas of health, education and training which aim to improve human capital.

reforms.description: The introduction of new legislation broadened the eligible investments for the Community Support Framework II (CSF II), improving the administration of public investment projects.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The reform enhances the efficiency of public investment, which can lead to better outcomes in innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The legislation has been enacted and is currently in force.
reforms.importance_rationale: While significant, this reform is more of an adjustment to existing frameworks rather than a complete overhaul.
reforms.source_quote: The implementation of public investment projects has suffered from delays due to administrative constraints and Greece has absorbed only about 55 per cent of targeted CSF funds... The improved performance during the second year has resulted... from the introduction of new legislation broadening the eligible investments and improving administration.

reforms.description: New initiatives to promote technology diffusion across industry include the establishment of programme brokers and schemes for Technology Performance Financing and Best Practice Benchmarking, alongside efforts to strengthen the links between universities and enterprises regarding research topics and funding.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: These initiatives are aimed at enhancing the transfer of knowledge and technology, which is essential for innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: These initiatives have been officially announced but are not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: While important for improving technology transfer, these initiatives are not as transformative as major reforms.
reforms.source_quote: Technology diffusion across industry, which is to be promoted by new programme brokers ('Technomisites') or specialised counsellors as well as by new schemes on Technology Performance Financing and Best Practice Benchmarking.

reforms.description: The Greek government introduced a programme to facilitate job search and placement, which includes a voucher system for the unemployed to apply towards training, wage subsidies, or unemployment benefits.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This programme aims to enhance the skills of the workforce, which is crucial for innovation and economic development.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme has been announced but is not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: While beneficial, this programme is an incremental adjustment rather than a major reform.
reforms.source_quote: In 1995 the Government presented an ambitious programme to facilitate job search and placement and improve the training of the unemployed.
